manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: pitch, gold, well, cook, australia, line, pass, turkey, palm.
Your opponent's team words are: fork, centaur, telescope, shakespeare, press, whip, foot, kangaroo.
Distractor words are: plastic, rome, server.
The kill words are: watch, pool, stock, swing, part.

Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

I need to give a clue for the single remaining word "well".

Let me think about the meanings of "well":
1. A water well (a hole in the ground to get water)
2. "Well" as in "doing well" (adverb meaning in a good way)
3. "Well done" (exclamation)
4. Oil well
5. Wishing well

I need to avoid words that might connect to:
- Opponent words: fork, telescope, press, kangaroo
- Distractor words: plastic, rome, server
- Kill words: watch, pool, stock, swing, part

Some clue ideas for "well":
- WATER (connects to well, but might connect to pool which is a kill word - risky!)
- OIL (oil well)
- DEEP (wells are deep)
- BUCKET (used with wells)
- WISH (wishing well)
- HEALTHY (doing well)
- GOOD (doing well, but might be too abstract)
- SPRING (water spring, well)
- HOLE (well is a hole)
- DRILL (drilling a well)

Let me evaluate the safest options:
- WATER is risky because of "pool" (kill word)
- BUCKET seems safe and clearly relates to well
- WISH is safe and relates to wishing well
- OIL could work for oil well
- DEEP might be too vague

I think BUCKET is a good choice - it's clearly associated with wells (water wells, drawing water from a well with a bucket), and doesn't seem to connect to any dangerous words.

Actually, let me reconsider WISH - wishing wells are a common concept. Does WISH connect to anything dangerous?
- fork, telescope, press, kangaroo - no clear connection
- plastic, rome, server - no clear connection
- watch, pool, stock, swing, part - no clear connection

WISH seems safe too.

Between BUCKET and WISH, I think BUCKET is more directly associated with the physical object of a well, while WISH is more about the specific type (wishing well). 

Both work, so I'll go with BUCKET since it's the more concrete and direct association.
